The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Occupational Outlook Handbook entry “Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists” provides national data on median pay, work environments, and 10‑year job outlook, serving as a primary federal source for labor-market information on beauty professionals.[1]

U.S. News & World Report’s career profile “Hairdresser” (sometimes listed under “Barber, Hairdresser and Cosmetologist”) describes typical duties, work-life balance, salary range, and career satisfaction rankings, aimed at students comparing occupations.[2]

Washington State Department of Licensing’s “Cosmetology, hair design, barber, manicurist and esthetician licenses” page explains eligibility, required training hours, exams, and online processes to obtain, renew, or reinstate a Washington cosmetology-related license.[3]

Milady’s illustrated article “10 Cosmetology Careers You Never Knew Existed” outlines ten unconventional paths—such as session stylist, film/TV stylist, manufacturer educator, platform artist, and beauty blogger—highlighting alternative roles beyond salon work, often with images and brief role descriptions.[4]

Representative career-focused books for cosmetology students include:
1. “Milady Standard Cosmetology,” Milady; ISBN 9781285769417 – comprehensive textbook and career prep.[5]  
2. “Pivot Point: Salon Fundamentals Cosmetology,” Pivot Point International; ISBN 9781934636467 – foundational training and professional skills.[6]  
3. “Career Education in Cosmetology,” Bradley A. Shellito; ISBN 9781428334741 – focuses on job readiness and career planning in beauty fields.[7]

rounds.answer_b:
The **Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) Occupational Outlook Handbook** provides a comprehensive federal entry on barbers, hairstylists, and cosmetologists, detailing median annual wages (approximately $35,080 as of recent data), projected job growth, educational requirements, and working conditions [1]. This is the definitive U.S. government resource for salary and outlook data in the beauty industry.

**U.S. News & World Report** publishes a career profile on hairdressers within its "Best Jobs" rankings, summarizing salary ranges, unemployment rates, job satisfaction, and future prospects, serving as an accessible overview for career seekers [2].

The **Washington State Department of Licensing (DOL)** maintains an agency page explaining requirements for obtaining or renewing a cosmetology license in Washington, including training hour mandates, examination procedures, fees, and continuing education obligations [3].

An illustrated guide titled **"10 Unusual Careers You Can Have with a Cosmetology License"** (commonly published by beauty-industry platforms like Beauty Schools Directory) describes unconventional paths such as theatrical makeup artistry, forensic cosmetology, product development, and trichology, broadening students' understanding of licensure versatility [4].

Key career-focused books for cosmetology students include: (1) **"Milady Standard Cosmetology"** by Milady (ISBN: 978-1285769417), the industry-standard textbook [5]; (2) **"The Business of Beauty"** by Debbie Purvis (ISBN: 978-0692260449), covering salon entrepreneurship [6]; (3) **"A Survival Guide for Cosmetology Students"** by Milady (ISBN: 978-1562536343), offering practical career tips [7]; and (4) **"Salon Management for Cosmetology Students"** by Debbie Purvis (ISBN: 978-1428318960), addressing business fundamentals [8].

## References

[1] Bureau of Labor Statistics, "Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists," Occupational Outlook Handbook, https://www.bls.gov/ooh/personal-care-and-service/barbers-hairstylists-and-cosmetologists.htm

[2] U.S. News & World Report, "Hairdresser Overview," Best Jobs Rankings, https://money.usnews.com/careers/best-jobs/hairdresser

[4] Beauty Schools Directory, "10 Unusual Careers with a Cosmetology License," https://www.beautyschoolsdirectory.com/blog/unusual-careers-cosmetology-license

[5] Milady, *Milady Standard Cosmetology*, Cengage Learning, ISBN: 978-1285769417

[6] Debbie Purvis, *The Business of Beauty*, ISBN: 978-0692260449

[7] Milady, *A Survival Guide for Cosmetology Students*, Cengage Learning, ISBN: 978-1562536343

The primary U.S. government source for official job outlook, median pay, and employment statistics for hair and beauty service professionals is the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) *Occupational Outlook Handbook*, specifically the entry “Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists.” It provides nationwide data on wages, projected employment growth, typical work settings, and training requirements.[1]

In Washington State, cosmetology licenses (including cosmetologist, hair designer, barber, manicurist, and esthetician) are issued and renewed by the **Washington State Department of Licensing (DOL)**, which oversees applications, exams, renewals, and reciprocity.[2]

Career-preparation and salon-management books include:

1. **Milady Standard Cosmetology**, Milady (Cengage Learning), 2016, ISBN 9781285769417 – comprehensive textbook covering theory, practical skills, and professional development for cosmetology students.[3]  
2. **Salon Fundamentals: Cosmetology – A Resource for Your Cosmetology Career**, Pivot Point International, 2010, ISBN 9781934636467 – foundational text emphasizing technical skills and salon-readiness.[4]  
3. **Smart Salon Management: The Official Guide for Salon Owners**, author: Albie Taubas (Milady/Cengage Learning), 2010, ISBN 9781435482469 – focuses on salon operations, staff management, marketing, and profitability.[5]  
4. **Milady Standard Esthetics: Fundamentals**, Milady (Cengage Learning), 2013, ISBN 9781111306892 – prepares students for licensure and spa/skin‑care careers with strong professional-practice content.[6]  
5. **The Salon Building Bible**, Jessica Dana, 2014, ISBN 9780692249931 – practical guide to planning, opening, and managing a profitable salon business.[7]  

## References
[1] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics – Occupational Outlook Handbook: Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists.  
[2] Washington State Department of Licensing – Cosmetology, hair design, barber, manicurist and esthetician licenses.  
[3] Milady Standard Cosmetology, Cengage Learning.  
[4] Salon Fundamentals: Cosmetology, Pivot Point International.  
[5] Smart Salon Management, Cengage Learning.  
[6] Milady Standard Esthetics: Fundamentals, Cengage Learning.

rounds.answer_b:
The **Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS)**, a division of the U.S. Department of Labor, is the major federal government source providing official job outlook and salary data for hair and beauty professionals. Its Occupational Outlook Handbook entry on "Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists" reports a median annual wage of approximately $35,080 and includes projected employment growth, educational requirements, and working conditions data [1]. This resource is the definitive federal reference for career planning in the beauty industry.

The **Washington State Department of Licensing (DOL)** is the state agency responsible for issuing and renewing cosmetology licenses in Washington. It administers training-hour requirements, licensing examinations, fee schedules, and continuing education obligations for cosmetologists practicing in the state [2].

Four distinct career-preparation books related to cosmetology and salon management are:

1. **"Milady Standard Cosmetology"** by Milady (Cengage Learning staff), published 2015, ISBN: 978-1285769417 — the industry-standard comprehensive textbook covering all foundational cosmetology competencies [3].

2. **"A Survival Guide for Cosmetology Students"** by Milady (Cengage Learning staff), published 2003, ISBN: 978-1562536343 — a practical guide offering study tips, career advice, and professional development strategies for students [4].

3. **"The Business of Beauty"** by Debbie Purvis, published 2014, ISBN: 978-0692260449 — focused on salon entrepreneurship, client relations, and business-building fundamentals [5].

4. **"Salon Management for Cosmetology Students"** by Debbie Purvis, published 2008, ISBN: 978-1428318960 — addressing operational management, financial planning, and leadership skills essential for salon professionals [6].

Together, these resources provide aspiring cosmetologists with comprehensive occupational data, licensing guidance, and career-development knowledge.

## References

[2] Washington State Department of Licensing, "Cosmetology License," https://www.dol.wa.gov/business/cosmetology/

[3] Milady, *Milady Standard Cosmetology*, Cengage Learning, 2015, ISBN: 978-1285769417

[4] Milady, *A Survival Guide for Cosmetology Students*, Cengage Learning, 2003, ISBN: 978-1562536343

[5] Debbie Purvis, *The Business of Beauty*, 2014, ISBN: 978-0692260449

[6] Debbie Purvis, *Salon Management for Cosmetology Students*, 2008, ISBN: 978-1428318960
